inclusions: Heading 39.01 — Polymers of ethylene in primary forms: LDPE, HDPE, LLDPE, VLDPE; EVA (ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymers ≥95%); ethylene-alpha-olefin copolymers; liquid synthetic polyolefins with <60% vol distilling at 300°C
Heading 39.02 — Polymers of propylene or of other olefins in primary forms: polypropylene (PP); polyisobutylene; propylene copolymers; other polyolefins
Heading 39.03 — Polymers of styrene in primary forms: polystyrene (PS), expandable; ABS (acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene ≥95%); SAN (styrene-acrylonitrile ≥95%); other styrene copolymers

Heading 39.04 — Polymers of vinyl chloride or other halogenated olefins in primary forms: PVC (polyvinyl chloride); vinyl chloride-vinyl acetate copolymers (≥95%); PVDC (polyvinylidene chloride); PTFE, FEP and other fluoropolymers; vinyl chloride copolymers

Heading 39.05 — Polymers of vinyl acetate or other vinyl esters in primary forms; other vinyl polymers: PVA (polyvinyl acetate); PVOH (polyvinyl alcohol); PVB (polyvinyl butyral); other vinyl polymers
Heading 39.06 — Acrylic polymers in primary forms: PMMA (polymethyl methacrylate); polyacrylic acid; polyacrylates; polyacrylamide; acrylonitrile polymers; acrylic copolymers

Heading 39.07 — Polyacetals, other polyethers and epoxide resins in primary forms; polycarbonates, alkyd resins, polyallyl esters and other polyesters in primary forms: POM (polyacetal/polyoxymethylene); PEG/PPG (polyethylene/propylene glycols); epoxy resins; PET/PBT/PTT (polyesters); polycarbonate (PC); alkyd resins
Heading 39.08 — Polyamides in primary forms: nylon-6; nylon-6,6; nylon-11; nylon-12; other polyamides

Heading 39.09 — Amino-resins, phenolic resins and polyurethanes in primary forms: urea-formaldehyde (UF) resins; melamine-formaldehyde (MF) resins; phenol-formaldehyde (PF) resins; polyurethanes (PU) — rigid and flexible; resols and other prepolymers
Heading 39.10 — Silicones in primary forms: polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS); silicone oils, emulsions, resins in primary form

Heading 39.11 — Petroleum resins, coumarone-indene resins, polyterpenes, polysulphides, polysulphones and other products specified in Note 3 of this Chapter, not elsewhere specified or included, in primary forms. Includes: hydrocarbon resins; coumarone-indene resins even if not highly polymerised; poly(2,6-dimethyl-1,4-phenylene oxide); poly(phenylene sulphide)

Heading 39.12 — Cellulose and its chemical derivatives in primary forms: cellulose acetate; cellulose acetate-butyrate; cellulose nitrate; CMC (carboxymethylcellulose); HPMC; ethylcellulose; methylcellulose; viscose
Heading 39.13 — Natural polymers (e.g., alginic acid) and modified natural polymers (e.g., hardened proteins, chemical derivatives of natural rubber) in primary forms, NES
Heading 39.14 — Ion-exchangers based on polymers of headings 39.01 to 39.13, in primary forms
Heading 39.15 — Waste, parings and scrap of plastics. NOTE: excludes waste of a single thermoplastic material converted back into primary forms (→ 39.01–39.14 per Note 7)
Heading 39.16 — Monofilament of which any cross-sectional dimension exceeds 1 mm, rods, sticks and profile shapes, of plastics

Heading 39.17 — Tubes, pipes and hoses and fittings therefor (e.g., joints, elbows, flanges) of plastics. Includes sausage casings and lay-flat tubing. Round/oval/rectangular/regular polygon cross-section required (except lay-flat; others → 39.16 profile shapes)

Heading 39.18 — Floor coverings of plastics, whether or not self-adhesive, in rolls or in the form of tiles; wall or ceiling coverings of plastics (Note 9: in rolls ≥45 cm wide, with decorated plastic face on non-paper backing)
Heading 39.19 — Self-adhesive plates, sheets, film, foil, tape, strip and other flat shapes, of plastics, whether or not in rolls
Heading 39.20 — Other plates, sheets, film, foil and strip, of plastics, NOT self-adhesive, NOT cellular, uncut or cut into rectangles only (Note 10). Combined with non-textile backing materials
Heading 39.21 — Other plates, sheets, film, foil and strip of plastics — cellular (foam) or combined with other materials (not eligible for 39.20)
Heading 39.22 — Baths, shower-baths, washbasins, bidets, lavatory pans, seats and covers, flushing cisterns and similar sanitary ware of plastics
Heading 39.23 — Articles for the conveyance or packing of goods of plastics: boxes, crates, trays, sacks, bags, containers; spools, bobbins, cops and similar supports
Heading 39.24 — Tableware, kitchenware, other household articles and hygienic or toilet articles of plastics

Heading 39.25 — Builders' ware of plastics NES (Note 11 exhaustive list): tanks/reservoirs/vats >300L; structural elements (floors, walls, ceilings, roofs); gutters; doors/windows/frames/thresholds; balconies/balustrades/fencing/gates; shutters/blinds; large-scale shelving for permanent installation; ornamental architectural features; fittings/mountings for permanent installation in buildings (knobs, handles, hooks, switch-plates)

Heading 39.26 — Other articles of plastics and articles of other materials of headings 39.01 to 39.14: stoppers, lids, caps; office/school supplies; clothing/apparel accessories; fittings for furniture, coachwork or the like; statuettes and other ornamental articles; transmission or conveyor belts or belting; other articles NES

exclusions.condition: Lubricating preparations of heading 27.10 (petroleum-based lubricants) or heading 34.03 (other lubricating preparations)
exclusions.action: Redirect to 27.10 or 34.03 even if based on polymer components (Ch.39 Note 2(a))

exclusions.condition: Waxes of heading 27.12 (mineral waxes: paraffin, ozokerite, petroleum wax) or heading 34.04 (artificial waxes: polyethylene glycol wax, silicone wax, prepared waxes)
Separate chemically defined organic compounds (even if monomers or polymeric in structure)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 29 — e.g., pure caprolactam (monomer for nylon-6) → 29.33; pure ethylene glycol → Ch.29; pure formaldehyde → Ch.29 (Ch.39 Note 2(c))

exclusions.condition: Heparin or its salts
Solutions (other than collodions) of polymers of headings 39.01–39.13 in volatile organic solvents when solvent weight >50% of solution weight
Stamping foils of heading 32.12
Organic surface-active agents or preparations of heading 34.02 (synthetic detergents/non-ionic/anionic/cationic surfactants based on polymers)
Run gums or ester gums of heading 38.06 (rosin-based materials)
Prepared additives for mineral oils or similar liquids of heading 38.11 (viscosity index improvers, pour point depressants — polymer-based for lube oil)
Prepared hydraulic fluids based on polyglycols, silicones or other polymers of Chapter 39 — heading 38.19
Diagnostic or laboratory reagents on a backing of plastics
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 38.22 (Ch.39 Note 2(k)) — plastic-backed test strips, lateral flow devices, microarray substrates

exclusions.condition: Synthetic rubber as defined for Chapter 40, or articles thereof
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 40 — Ch.40 Note 4 defines synthetic rubber as unsaturated synthetic substances that can be irreversibly vulcanised with sulphur (Ch.39 Note 2(l))

exclusions.condition: Saddlery or harness (42.01); trunks, suitcases, handbags, other containers of plastics (42.02)
Wall coverings of heading 48.14 (wallpaper — paper-backed, not meeting Note 9 criteria for 39.18)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 48.14 if the backing is paper (Ch.39 Note 2(o)). Plastic wall coverings on NON-PAPER backing ≥45cm wide → 39.18

exclusions.condition: Goods of Section XI — textiles and textile articles — even if coated, laminated or impregnated with plastics
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XI (Chapters 50–63) (Ch.39 Note 2(p)). Note 1 specifies 'plastics' does NOT apply to materials regarded as textile materials of Section XI

exclusions.condition: Footwear, headgear, umbrellas, walking-sticks (Section XII articles) made of plastic
Imitation jewellery of heading 71.17 (costume jewellery, fashion jewellery of plastics)
Articles of Section XVI — machines and electrical/mechanical appliances with plastic parts or housings, where the plastic is integral to defined machinery
exclusions.action: Redirect to relevant Chapter 84 or 85 heading for the machine/appliance (Ch.39 Note 2(s))

exclusions.condition: Parts of aircraft or vehicles of Section XVII (plastic bumpers, dashboards, body parts as parts of vehicles)
Optical elements, spectacle frames, drawing instruments of Chapter 90 made of plastics
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 90 (Ch.39 Note 2(u)) — plastic optical lenses → 90.01; plastic spectacle frames → 90.03

exclusions.condition: Furniture, luminaires, prefabricated buildings of Chapter 94
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 94 — plastic furniture → 94.01/94.03; plastic light fittings → 94.05 (Ch.39 Note 2(x))

exclusions.condition: Toys, games, sports equipment of Chapter 95
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 95 — plastic toys → 95.03; plastic sports equipment → 95.06 (Ch.39 Note 2(y))

exclusions.condition: Brushes, buttons, slide fasteners, combs, pens (Chapter 96 articles) made of plastics
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 96 — plastic buttons → 96.06; plastic zippers → 96.07; plastic combs → 96.15 (Ch.39 Note 2(z))

classification_rules.description: Plastics = materials of headings 39.01–39.14 capable (at polymerisation or later) of being formed under external influence (heat/pressure/solvent/plasticiser) into shapes that are RETAINED after removal of influence. Processes: moulding, casting, extruding, rolling. Includes vulcanised fibre. EXCLUDES materials regarded as textile materials of Section XI.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Headings 39.01–39.11 require chemical synthesis + specific categories
classification_rules.description: Headings 39.01–39.11 apply ONLY to chemically synthesised goods in these categories: (a) Liquid synthetic polyolefins with <60% vol distilling at 300°C (→ 39.01/39.02); (b) Coumarone-indene resins, not highly polymerised (→ 39.11); (c) Other synthetic polymers with average ≥5 monomer units; (d) Silicones (→ 39.10); (e) Resols and other prepolymers (→ 39.09).

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — Copolymer classification: predominant comonomer rule
classification_rules.description: COPOLYMERS (co-polycondensates, co-polyaddition products, block copolymers, graft copolymers) and POLYMER BLENDS → classified in the heading covering polymers of the COMONOMER UNIT THAT PREDOMINATES BY WEIGHT over every other single comonomer unit. If no single unit predominates → last heading in numerical order among equally qualifying headings. Note: constituent comonomer units of polymers already in the same heading are taken together.

classification_rules.rule: Note 5 — Chemically modified polymers classify with unmodified parent
classification_rules.description: Chemically modified polymers (where only appendages/side chains on the main polymer chain have been changed by chemical reaction) → classified in the SAME heading as the UNMODIFIED POLYMER. Exception: graft copolymers are NOT covered by this note (they are true copolymers applying Note 4).

Primary forms in headings 39.01–39.14 means ONLY: (a) Liquids and pastes, including dispersions (emulsions, suspensions) and solutions; (b) Blocks of irregular shape, lumps, powders (including moulding powders), granules, flakes and similar bulk forms. These are raw/intermediate polymer forms before fabrication into articles.

classification_rules.rule: Note 7 — Heading 39.15 (plastic waste) vs 39.01–39.14 (recycled primary forms)
classification_rules.description: Heading 39.15 (waste, parings, scrap of plastics) DOES NOT APPLY to waste of a single thermoplastic material that has already been CONVERTED BACK INTO PRIMARY FORMS. Such recycled/reprocessed single-resin granules, pellets or powders go back into headings 39.01–39.14 as primary forms.

Hollow plastic products for conveying gases/liquids → 39.17 if cross-section is: round, oval, rectangular (length ≤1.5× width), or regular polygon. EXCEPTION: lay-flat tubing and sausage casings always qualify regardless of shape. Products with OTHER cross-sections → 39.16 (profile shapes), NOT 39.17.

classification_rules.rule: Note 9 — Wall/ceiling coverings of plastics (39.18): must be in rolls ≥45cm, on non-paper backing
classification_rules.description: Plastic wall/ceiling coverings qualifying for heading 39.18 must be: (1) in ROLLS; (2) width ≥45 cm; (3) suitable for wall/ceiling decoration; (4) plastics PERMANENTLY FIXED on backing of any material OTHER THAN PAPER; (5) the plastic face layer must be grained, embossed, coloured, design-printed or otherwise decorated. Paper-backed equivalents → heading 48.14.

classification_rules.rule: Note 10 — Plates/sheets/film (39.20/39.21): uncut or cut rectangles only
classification_rules.description: Headings 39.20/39.21 apply ONLY to plates, sheets, film, foil and strip (not of Chapter 54) and blocks of regular geometric shape, whether or not printed/surface-worked, that are: UNCUT or CUT INTO RECTANGLES (including squares) — BUT NOT FURTHER WORKED. Even if the cut piece is ready for use as an article, it still classifies here. Further working (trimming to non-rectangular shape, punching, printing with specific markings, etc.) takes it to 39.26.

classification_rules.rule: Note 11 — Heading 39.25: builders' ware — exhaustive list, NES restriction
classification_rules.description: Heading 39.25 covers ONLY the specifically listed builders' plastic articles (see inclusions) AND only those NOT covered by earlier headings of sub-Chapter II. The NES (not elsewhere specified) condition means any plastic building article covered by 39.17–39.24 must first go there. Only uncovered builders' articles fall into 39.25.

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 1 — Polymer classification within a heading: 'poly-' prefix = ≥95% single monomer
classification_rules.description: Within a heading, a subheading naming a polymer with the prefix 'poly' (e.g., polyethylene, polyamide-6,6) requires that the named monomer unit(s) TOGETHER contribute ≥95% by weight of total polymer content. Specific copolymers named in subheadings 3901.30, 3901.40, 3903.20, 3903.30 and 3904.30 similarly require ≥95% of the named comonomer units. Chemically modified polymers that don't specifically fit → subheading 'Other'. Remaining polymers → subheading of the predominant comonomer by weight.

The critical material distinction: PLASTICS (Ch.39) = thermoplastic or thermosetting polymers formed under heat/pressure, retaining shape — cannot be irreversibly vulcanised with sulphur. SYNTHETIC RUBBER (Ch.40) = unsaturated synthetic polymers that CAN be irreversibly vulcanised with sulphur at room temperature and will not break on stretching to 3× original length. Key examples: PVC → Ch.39 (not rubber). EVA → Ch.39. SBR, NBR, EPDM, neoprene → Ch.40 (synthetic rubber). Silicone rubber (cured) → Ch.40; uncured silicone → Ch.39.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: plastics in primary forms (39.01–39.14) vs plastic articles (39.15–39.26)
classification_rules.description: Raw polymer material in primary form (granules, powder, pellets, liquid dispersion, blocks, lumps) → 39.01–39.14 (based on polymer type). Once the material is shaped/fabricated into semi-manufactures or finished articles → 39.15–39.26 (based on article form/use). The primary form definition (Note 6) is the dividing line.

classification_rules.rule: GIR priority: many plastic articles go to specific chapters, NOT Chapter 39
classification_rules.description: Chapter 39 Note 2 lists 22 exclusions (a)–(z). For PLASTIC ARTICLES, always check if the item is: furniture (94), toys (95), footwear (64), textiles (50–63), machines (84/85), vehicle parts (87), optical goods (90), or any Section XII article. If yes, the specific chapter overrides Chapter 39. Chapter 39 headings 39.23–39.26 are catch-all for plastic articles not captured by more specific chapters elsewhere.
